task: Audit mysql-only scheduler and job seams
acceptance_criteria: Current mysql-only dispatch assumptions are explicit across scheduler, jobs, and sink boundaries
notes: The critical mysql-only seams were confirmed in scheduler typing, CollectionJob payload shape, MySQL worker ownership, Redis queue naming, and ClickHouse mysql_metrics sink

acceptance_criteria: Scheduler and collection job domain can dispatch by engine without regressing MySQL worker flow
validation_command: uv run pytest tests/scheduler tests/integration/metrics_pipeline tests/worker_mysql
notes: CollectionJob now carries engine identity with backward-compatible JSON decoding, and scheduler dispatch is explicitly gated by supported collection engines while MySQL worker flow stays unchanged

acceptance_criteria: Open gaps between MySQL and Oracle pipeline capabilities are explicit
notes: Recorded explicit gap evidence: Oracle is intentionally skipped at dispatch, while queue naming, ClickHouse sink table, collector protocol, normalization, and worker remain MySQL-specific
